---
title: "Youth Aspirations and Resilience in the Post-COVID Era"
author: "Yonatan D. Enaro"
date: "2025-07-10"
categories: [Labor Economics, SurveyCTO]
---

Synthesizing findings from Ethiopia's component in a 6-country study on youth employment challenges and adaptive strategies.

## Background

The COVID-19 pandemic disrupted labor markets globally, with youth bearing a disproportionate share of job losses. Understanding how young people adapt requires nationally representative data and cross-national comparisons.

## Methodology

This analysis draws from the Africa Youth Aspirations & Resilience Survey (AYPReS), a 6-country study covering Ethiopia, Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, Uganda, and Tanzania. In Ethiopia, we surveyed 1,556 youth across 9 regions using SurveyCTO.

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
# Youth employment analysis
tabulate employment_status age_group, row chi2
* Employment sector shifts post-COVID
graph bar (mean) employment_rate, over(year) ...
```

## Key Findings

- 34% of youth reported income loss during the pandemic
- Self-employment increased by 12 percentage points
- Digital literacy emerged as a key resilience factor
- Female youth faced double the employment shock

## Policy Implications

Policymakers should prioritize digital skills training, youth entrepreneurship support, and gender-targeted interventions to build back better.
